mnemonics: CPU-VI: Compute, Partition, Unify - Virtualize, Isolate
keyPoints: Hypervisor manages physical resources and allocates them to VMs
Virtual CPUs are time-sliced shares of physical CPU cores
Memory virtualization uses shadow page tables or hardware-assisted virtualization
I/O devices are virtualized through emulation, paravirtualization, or direct assignment
commonMistakes: Assuming virtualization overhead is negligible for all workloads
Over-provisioning virtual CPUs leading to performance degradation
Not considering NUMA architecture when allocating memory to VMs
